name: Tomasz Barański
relationship: best friend since university, both studied economics at UEK Kraków 2006-2011
source: university friend, same dorm floor freshman year
context: Runs his own agency in Kielce, focuses on commercial properties. We talk almost every day — he's the person I call when a deal feels off or when I need to vent. His wife Ania is a pediatrician. They have two kids, Zosia (9) and Mikołaj (6). We go fishing together twice a year, usually Mazury in July.
last_interaction_summary: Called me Sunday evening to talk through a strip mall deal he's nervous about — seller is hiding something with the environmental report.
tags: close friend, real estate, commercial, fishing, Kielce
notes: Zosia's birthday May 3. I told Tomek I'd look into that lakeside plot near Giżycko he wants for a summer house — haven't started.

name: Piotr Zieliński
relationship: mortgage broker, primary business partner since 2019
source: introduced by Jakub Wójcik at a closing in 2019
context: Handles 80% of my clients' financing. Incredibly reliable, knows every product on the market. Recently started doing commercial mortgage too, which opens doors for us. Divorced last year, going through a rough patch. Has a daughter, Lena (12), who he sees every other weekend.
last_interaction_summary: Quick midweek call — he flagged that Credit Agricole tightened their 2% down criteria and Paweł Adamczyk's case might not fit anymore. Said he'd find a workaround.
notes: Piotr's been hinting he wants to formalize our referral arrangement — maybe a written partnership agreement. I keep dodging it. Should decide.

name: Karolina Nowicka
relationship: sister, also does staging and interior design freelance
source: family
context: Three years younger, lives 15 minutes away. Started a home staging side business two years ago and she's genuinely good at it — I use her for most of my listings now. Married to Bartek who works at the Volvo dealer. Their son Olek just turned 4.
last_interaction_summary: She dropped off fabric samples for the Kamińska Street listing staging and stayed for coffee.
notes: Bartek's 40th birthday party is May 22 — Karolina is planning a surprise, I need to keep my mouth shut. She asked me to help coordinate the venue.

name: Jakub Wójcik
relationship: lawyer handling real estate closings, working together since 2017
source: met through Andrzej Nowak when we were both at Nieruchomości Centrum
context: Runs a two-person law office downtown. Meticulous, sometimes painfully slow, but I've never had a contract issue with his work. His wife Magda teaches at the primary school. They're expecting their third kid in June.
last_interaction_summary: Sunday phone call — he wanted to vent about a closing that fell apart and check in. Magda's nesting hard, due in six weeks.
notes: Magda is due mid-June. I should get them something — last time I forgot and felt terrible.

name: Anna Krajewska
relationship: former client turned close friend, bought her apartment through me in 2020
source: cold walk-in to the office in 2020, looking for a two-bedroom in Śródmieście
context: Sold her a beautiful apartment on Paderewskiego street. She works as a marketing manager at a mid-size cosmetics company, Laboratorium Kosmetyczne. Has referred four or five clients to me over the years — she's my best referral source, hands down. Single mom, daughter Hania (7).
last_interaction_summary: She came over for coffee Sunday afternoon — Hania played in the garden. Anna pushed me again about her CEO's apartment search; I finally promised to send three options by end of week.
notes: She casually mentioned that her boss, the CEO of Laboratorium Kosmetyczne, is looking for a 200sqm apartment — luxury segment. I said I'd be in touch. NEED to follow up on this, it could be a huge deal.

name: Damian Krawczyk
relationship: childhood friend, general contractor
source: grew up on the same street in Pińczów
context: We've known each other since we were five. He runs a small renovation crew — four guys — and does most of the pre-sale renovations I recommend to sellers. Hard worker, sometimes overcommits and timelines slip. Wife Kasia, two teenagers.
last_interaction_summary: Stopped by the Wiśniowa Street site Friday morning — bathroom is finally done. Damian looked tired, mentioned the back is bad again. Still no invoice for the Kowalczyk job.
notes: Damian still hasn't invoiced me for the Kowalczyk job from February. Reminded him twice. Also: he's been complaining about his back again — might need surgery this year.

name: Beata Mazur
relationship: current client, selling family home on Sienkiewicza street
source: referred by Teresa Głowacka from BNI
context: Widow, early 60s. Selling the family home after her husband passed last year. It's emotional for her — three kids grew up there. The house needs work but the location is prime. She's planning to buy a smaller apartment afterward, so it's two transactions.
last_interaction_summary: Showed the house to two prospective buyers over the weekend. She called Monday morning, anxious about the feedback.
notes: She keeps asking about whether the parking spot comes with the property or not — I told her I'd check the land registry two weeks ago and still haven't. The daughter Marta lives in Warsaw and calls me directly sometimes, which is a bit much.

name: Marek Kowalski
relationship: client building a rental portfolio, met in 2023
source: came to an open house I was running on Złota street in 2023
context: Mid-40s, works in logistics at InPost. Bought his first investment apartment through me in 2024, now wants a second. Very analytical — spreadsheets for everything. Interested in short-term rental arbitrage. His wife Bożena is skeptical about the whole real estate investing thing.
last_interaction_summary: Sent him three listings in the university district — good rental yield. He wants to see them next week.
notes: Viewings booked for next Tuesday. Bring the rental yield comparison Dorota helped me build last month — he'll appreciate it.

name: Stefan Fischer
relationship: German expat client, bought apartment in 2024
source: found me through Google search for English-speaking agents
context: Works for Continental in their local plant. Bought a two-bedroom near Rondo Mogilskie in 2024. Wife Julia and baby daughter. Speaks decent Polish now. Recently mentioned wanting to buy a second place as an investment before they potentially move back to Stuttgart.
last_interaction_summary: Coffee at Nowa Prowincja — he asked about property tax implications for non-resident owners. I didn't know the answer and said I'd check.
notes: Told him I'd research the tax situation for non-residents and get back to him. That was three weeks ago.

name: Ewa Szymańska
relationship: notary public, professional relationship since 2019
source: met at chamber of commerce mixer in 2019
context: Her office handles most of my closings that aren't at the seller's preferred notary. Fast, professional, great with nervous first-time buyers. She just moved her office to the new building on Starowiślna.
last_interaction_summary: Closing for the Górski apartment — smooth as always. She asked if I know anyone selling commercial space because she wants to buy her office instead of renting.
notes: She's looking to buy her office space — 80-100sqm commercial in Kazimierz or Starowiślna area. Should keep an eye out.

name: Robert Jankowski
relationship: property photographer, working together since 2021
source: found him on Instagram — his architectural photography caught my eye
context: Does all my listing photography and drone shots. Quick turnaround, good eye. Also does wedding photography on weekends which sometimes creates scheduling conflicts. Quiet guy, hard to read, but his work speaks for itself.
last_interaction_summary: Shot the Sienkiewicza listing (Beata Mazur's house). Delivered next-day as promised.
notes: Need to ask if he can move the Wieliczka shoot to a weekday — he mentioned a wedding that Saturday in March.

name: Agnieszka Lis
relationship: runs staging and cleaning company, Czysto i Pięknie
source: referred by Karolina Nowicka in 2022
context: Started as a cleaning service, pivoted to also doing basic staging when she saw the demand. I use her for listings where full staging is overkill. She's ambitious — wants to expand into full property management. Recently hired two new employees.
last_interaction_summary: She did a deep clean and light staging for the Wiśniowa Street property. Did a great job, asked me to review her company on Google.
notes: I owe her dinner — she came in on a Sunday to emergency-prep a listing when a buyer wanted to see it Monday morning. Haven't followed through. Also she asked me to introduce her to Dorota Kamińska for property management work — I said I would.

name: Krzysztof Pawlak
relationship: residential developer, small to mid-size projects
source: met at a Targi Nieruchomości trade show in Kraków, 2021
context: Builds 10-20 unit residential projects, mostly on the south side of the city. We've done three projects together where I handled sales. Good quality builds, sometimes cuts it close on timelines. Currently building a 16-unit project on Zakopiańska. His business partner is Grzegorz Jabłoński, though I think Grzegorz went to do his own thing.
last_interaction_summary: Meeting to discuss sales strategy for the Zakopiańska project — he wants me to handle all 16 units exclusively.
notes: The Zakopiańska project is his biggest yet — he's nervous about it. 4 units already pre-sold. If I take this exclusively it's a major commitment but could be 200k+ in commission.

name: Monika Wiśniewska
relationship: mortgage broker, secondary contact when Piotr is overloaded
source: met at a bank training seminar in 2022
context: Works independently, good with non-standard cases — self-employed borrowers, foreign income, that kind of thing. Cheerful, always responsive on WhatsApp. She's also licensed in Germany which is unusual.
last_interaction_summary: Referred Stefan Fischer to her for the non-resident tax and mortgage question since Piotr doesn't do international cases.

name: Paweł Adamczyk
relationship: first-time buyer, current client
source: walked into the office in February 2026 after seeing a listing online
context: Young couple — Paweł and Iga, both 28. He's a software developer, she's a nurse. Looking for a two-bedroom apartment, budget around 450k. Nervous but excited. They're getting married in September and want to be settled before the wedding.
last_interaction_summary: Showed them two apartments on Podgórze — they loved the one on Limanowskiego but it's 30k over budget. Piotr is checking if the new Credit Agricole program could bridge the gap.
notes: Wedding is September 12. Iga's parents are contributing 50k toward the down payment. Paweł keeps asking about smart home stuff — he wants to wire everything.

name: Dorota Kamińska
relationship: landlord with 7 rental units, occasional seller
source: met through BNI chapter in 2020
context: Retired school principal, now manages her rental portfolio full-time. Shrewd businesswoman. Sells a unit every couple of years when she wants to rebalance, buys a new one. Currently thinking about switching some units to short-term rental. Has a son in London who manages her UK investments.
last_interaction_summary: She called to discuss converting her Kazimierz apartment to Airbnb. Wants me to run numbers on whether it's worth it vs. long-term tenant. Also mentioned she might sell the Nowa Huta unit.
tags: active client, landlord, rental portfolio, Airbnb, Kazimierz
notes: Promised her I'd send a short-term rental yield comparison for Kazimierz — two weeks ago. Haven't done it. Her son Tomek in London works at Savills as a commercial property analyst — could be a useful connection.

name: Michał Grabowski
relationship: fellow real estate agent at KW Nieruchomości, friendly competitor
source: worked together briefly at Nieruchomości Centrum in 2018-2019 before I went independent
context: Sharp agent, good closer. We sometimes bump into each other on listings and it can get competitive, but we generally respect each other's turf. He specializes in new builds from big developers — Murapol, Atal, that tier. His wife Ola is a dentist.
last_interaction_summary: Ran into him at a viewing on Wielicka — we were both showing clients the same unit. He got there first. We laughed about it afterward over coffee.

name: Katarzyna Dąbrowska
relationship: insurance agent, cross-referral partner since 2021
source: introduced by Adam Borkowski at BNI
context: She insures most of my clients' new purchases — home insurance, life insurance for mortgage requirements. Reliable, doesn't oversell. We have an informal referral exchange — I send her clients, she sends me people looking to buy.
last_interaction_summary: She referred a young couple to me — haven't called them yet. Also renewed insurance on my own office.
notes: The couple she referred — Nowak, I think? — I should call them this week. She texted me their number on March 25.

name: Łukasz Tomczak
relationship: property appraiser, professional contact since 2020
source: met through Piotr Zieliński
context: Does bank-ordered appraisals for mortgage applications. Fast turnaround, reasonable with valuations — some appraisers lowball everything. Quiet, professional, no-nonsense.
last_interaction_summary: Appraisal for the Podgórze apartment Paweł Adamczyk is interested in — came in at 460k which helps.

name: Sandra Vogel
relationship: Austrian investor, bought two apartments through me in 2023-2024
source: referred by Henrik Larsson at a PropTech conference in Vienna, 2022
context: Runs a small property investment company based in Salzburg. Bought two apartments in the Old Town for short-term rental. Very numbers-driven, communicates mainly by email, precise and formal. Her portfolio also includes properties in Budapest and Prague.
last_interaction_summary: Email exchange about occupancy rates for her two units. She's considering a third purchase if yields hold.
notes: She mentioned a colleague from her Vienna investment circle who wants to explore the Polish market — asked if I'd be open to an introduction. I said yes but haven't followed up.

name: Artur Michalski
relationship: IT consultant, built my website and CRM setup
source: found him on a local freelancers Facebook group in 2021
context: Set up my website, helped me with SEO, and tried to get me using a CRM two years ago (I gave up after a month). He's a solo consultant, works mostly with small businesses. Big into PropTech and AI tools — always sending me articles about how technology will change real estate.
last_interaction_summary: He pinged me about a new AI listing description tool he thinks I should try. I said I'd look at it, haven't.
notes: Still using the CRM he set up as basically a contact list. He'd be horrified. He also mentioned he's building a side project — some kind of property valuation tool using AI. Asked if I'd beta test it.

name: Natalia Kwiatkowska
relationship: current client, young couple buying first home
source: referred by Anna Krajewska — Natalia works with Anna at Laboratorium Kosmetyczne
context: She and her partner Olek are looking for a house with a garden in the suburbs — Wieliczka or Niepołomice direction. She's in marketing at Laboratorium Kosmetyczne under Anna. Very organized and communicates well. They have a golden retriever they keep mentioning as a factor in the garden requirement.
last_interaction_summary: Showed them a house in Wieliczka — they liked it but worried about the commute. Olek works at the Kraków Technology Park.
notes: Natalia mentioned she's pregnant — very early, asked me not to tell Anna yet since they work together. This explains the urgency to find a house.

name: James O'Brien
relationship: Irish expat, relocated to Kraków for Aon, bought apartment in 2024
source: found me through the Kraków Expats Facebook group
context: Works in IT risk management at Aon. Bought a one-bedroom on Dietla. Good guy, drinks Żywiec not Guinness as he likes to point out. Single. Talks about maybe buying a second place or moving to something bigger.
last_interaction_summary: Bumped into him at a pub. He mentioned his lease at work is up in 2027 and he's not sure if he'll stay in Poland.

name: Renata Szewczyk
relationship: community manager, runs the Podgórze Residents Facebook group (12k members)
source: met at a neighborhood council meeting in 2022
context: She's the unofficial mayor of Podgórze — knows everyone, organizes community events, runs the biggest local Facebook group. Works part-time at the library. Having her on my side means my Podgórze listings get organic visibility. She genuinely cares about the neighborhood.
last_interaction_summary: She posted about new bike lanes on Limanowskiego and I commented. She DM'd to ask if I'd write a short piece about the property market in Podgórze for her group — local perspective, not salesy.
tags: community, Podgórze, social media, local influence
notes: I said I'd write that piece for her Facebook group. That was a month ago. Haven't even started.

name: Wojciech Kowalczyk
relationship: past client, sold his house through me in late 2023
source: referral from Damian Krawczyk — they're neighbors in Pińczów area
context: Retired police officer, moved to a smaller apartment in the city after his wife passed. The sale took longer than expected and the final price was 40k below what I'd initially estimated, which he wasn't happy about. We parted on OK terms but not great.
last_interaction_summary: He texted to ask for a copy of some document from the sale. I sent it the next day. The exchange was polite but cool.

name: Magdalena Olszewska
relationship: past client, bought apartment in 2023
source: found me through OtoDom listing in 2023
context: Bought a studio apartment on Starowiślna as an investment. She's a pharmacist, quiet, very detail-oriented. Was happy with the purchase. Single, mid-30s.
last_interaction_summary: She emailed asking about property management options for her unit since she's thinking about renting it out. I suggested a few options but didn't follow through.

name: Andrzej Nowak
relationship: ex-colleague from Nieruchomości Centrum, 2016-2019
source: worked together for three years at the same agency
context: He stayed at Nieruchomości Centrum when I left to go independent in 2019. Things were tense — I took some clients with me, and he felt I should have given him a share. He's still there, now managing the office. Supposedly doing well.
last_interaction_summary: Ran into him at a notary office. Made small talk for two minutes. Stiff but civil.
notes: Still owes me an apology in my head — I don't owe him one. The 'share' he wanted was the three clients I'd personally brought into the agency. I run into him maybe twice a year. Always slightly cold.

name: Iwona Zawadzka
relationship: ex-colleague from Nieruchomości Centrum, 2017-2019, stayed friends
source: worked together at Nieruchomości Centrum
context: Left the agency about a year after I did. She's now doing property management full-time — doesn't sell anymore. We have coffee every few months. She's easygoing, knows the rental market inside out. Recently went through a divorce.
last_interaction_summary: Coffee at Bunkier. She's doing better — divorce is almost finalized. I finally introduced her to Dorota Kamińska for property management; they had lunch last week and clicked.
tags: ex-colleague, property management, rental market, Nieruchomości Centrum
notes: Iwona asked if I could keep an ear out for property management clients for her. I said yes — Dorota Kamińska and Agnieszka Lis both came to mind but I never made the connection.

name: Grzegorz Jabłoński
relationship: former business partner of Krzysztof Pawlak, now VP of Development at Atal
source: met at Targi Nieruchomości trade show in 2021, through Krzysztof
context: Used to build small projects with Krzysztof, then got poached by Atal in 2024. Now VP overseeing their south Poland developments — Kraków, Katowice, Wrocław. Big jump. We got along well at the trade show and a few dinners after, but I haven't kept in touch since he moved to Atal.
last_interaction_summary: Finally had that dinner — caught up at Pod Aniołami. He confirmed Atal is piloting external agent partnerships in Kraków and said he'd put me on the list when they formalize it.
notes: He's in a position to channel serious volume my way if Atal ever uses external agents for sales. The pilot is real — don't let this go cold.

name: Aleksandra Pietrzak
relationship: past client, bought a house in 2022
source: referral from Anna Krajewska
context: Bought a family home in Wieliczka. Her husband Tomek works in construction. Two young kids. She was easy to work with, referred one client to me afterward (I think it was Natalia Kwiatkowska, indirectly through Anna).
last_interaction_summary: Finally went to see her parents' Nowa Huta apartment. Solid condition, decent location. Gave her a valuation range; they want to think it over and probably list in autumn.
notes: Never went to look at her parents' place. That was nine months ago. Embarrassing.

name: Marcin Rutkowski
relationship: electrician, used him for several renovation projects 2020-2023
source: recommended by Damian Krawczyk
context: Good electrician, reliable quality but started getting expensive. I stopped calling him as much when Damian brought a cheaper guy onto his crew. Marcin might have noticed. He also does smart home installations which is a growing niche.
last_interaction_summary: Called him to ask about smart home wiring costs for a client. He was helpful but the quote was high.

name: Henrik Larsson
relationship: Swedish PropTech investor, met at PropTech Summit in Stockholm 2022
source: sat next to each other at the conference dinner
context: Runs a small fund — Scandinavian Property Ventures — that invests in CEE property and PropTech startups. Connected me with Sandra Vogel. Fascinating guy, speaks five languages. Very bullish on the Polish market.
last_interaction_summary: Long video call — he's in Stockholm, fund just closed a new raise. Asked if I'd host a small group of his LPs for a Kraków market tour later this year. Said yes.
tags: PropTech, investor, Swedish, CEE markets, fund
notes: He'd be a gold mine for introductions to serious international investors. His fund has apparently grown significantly — saw something about a new raise on LinkedIn.

name: Zofia Krajewska
relationship: Anna Krajewska's mother, helped her rent out a spare room in 2021
source: introduced by Anna Krajewska
context: Retired teacher, lovely woman. Lives alone in a large apartment on Karmelicka since her husband died. Anna convinced her to rent out one room to a student. I helped with the lease and basic setup. She bakes the best sernik I've ever had.
last_interaction_summary: She called to wish me happy name day (she always remembers). We chatted for ten minutes about Hania starting school.

name: Patryk Sobczak
relationship: local restaurant owner, past client
source: his restaurant Zielony Targ caters our BNI meetings, became a client in 2022
context: Bought a small commercial space for his second restaurant location through me in 2022. Energetic, always hustling. His restaurant Zielony Targ is a neighborhood institution. He's been talking about opening a third location.
last_interaction_summary: Lunch at Zielony Targ. The third location is real now — he's narrowed it down to two spaces in Zabłocie and wants my opinion on both this month.
tags: restaurant owner, commercial, Kazimierz, Zabłocie, expansion
notes: He's been talking about the third location for a year now. Might be getting close — saw him posting about visiting other cities' food scenes on Instagram. When he moves on this it could be a quick commercial deal.

name: Elżbieta Wróblewska
relationship: past client, downsized from house to apartment in 2023
source: referral from Ewa Szymańska, the notary
context: Retired teacher, early 70s. Sold her house in Krowodrza and bought a two-bedroom apartment closer to her daughter. Very kind, very particular. Took ages to decide on everything but was grateful in the end. Knits sweaters for everyone — I got one for Christmas 2023.
last_interaction_summary: She sent me a Christmas card (arriving in mid-January as usual) with a note about how much she loves the new apartment.

name: Kamil Chmielewski
relationship: young agent I mentored informally in 2023-2024
source: he emailed me asking for advice after reading my blog post about going independent
context: Was eager, green, had some talent. I spent a few hours with him over coffee giving him pointers. He was at a small agency in Wieliczka. Last I heard he was thinking about leaving real estate altogether — got frustrated with the income instability.
last_interaction_summary: He messaged on WhatsApp saying he's enrolled in a programming bootcamp. Wants to switch to IT. Asked if I know anyone hiring juniors.
notes: He asked if I know anyone in IT who could advise him. I should connect him with Artur Michalski or Paweł Adamczyk — both are developers. Never did.

name: Joanna Sikora
relationship: bank branch manager at PKO BP, professional contact since 2020
source: met through Piotr Zieliński
context: She manages the branch where many of my clients get their mortgages. Used to be a good relationship — she'd fast-track my clients' applications. Then last year she steered a client of mine toward a different agent for the purchase, claiming they wanted 'a more experienced agent' for a luxury listing. It was a 2M PLN sale. I was furious.
last_interaction_summary: Saw her at a chamber of commerce event. Nodded hello, nothing more.
notes: I think I need to call her. The Maciejewski thing was 18 months ago. Some clients are using PKO again and the relationship matters more than the grudge.

name: Adam Borkowski
relationship: fellow BNI chapter member since 2020, runs a printing company
source: joined the same BNI chapter — Kraków Business Leaders
context: Owns a mid-size printing company. Good networker, always passing referrals. He's the one who brought Katarzyna Dąbrowska into the chapter. We occasionally grab a beer after meetings. His company does all my marketing materials — brochures, business cards, signage.
last_interaction_summary: Last BNI meeting — he presented a new large-format printing service. Afterward he mentioned his daughter is apartment-hunting and asked if I could help.
tags: BNI, printing, marketing materials, referral partner
notes: His daughter Julia (24) needs a studio or small one-bedroom, budget 300-350k. Central or Podgórze. I should call her.

name: Teresa Głowacka
relationship: fellow BNI member, accountant specializing in small businesses
source: BNI Kraków Business Leaders chapter
context: Runs a small accounting firm. Very connected in the local business community. She's the one who referred Beata Mazur to me. Straightforward, no-nonsense, always wears red.
last_interaction_summary: BNI meeting — she did a 10-minute presentation about tax changes for 2026 that affect property investors. Useful stuff.

name: Szymon Kaczmarek
relationship: fellow BNI member, owns a used car dealership
source: BNI Kraków Business Leaders chapter
context: Big personality. Has a dealership on Zakopiańska. Not an obvious connection to real estate but he's referred two clients to me — people buying cars often also buying apartments, apparently. Likes to tell long stories.
last_interaction_summary: BNI meeting. He cornered me afterward to tell me about his trip to Dubai. Mentioned he's thinking about investing in an apartment — 'something nice, for Airbnb.'
notes: Mentioned he's serious about a 2-bedroom in the center for Airbnb after Dubai trip. Said he'd call me — he hasn't. Should follow up; he's good for it.

name: Mateusz Zając
relationship: past client, bought a townhouse in Niepołomice in 2023
source: found me through a Google ad
context: Engineer at Comarch, late 30s. Bought a new townhouse for his family — wife Paulina, two kids. Very satisfied with the process, left me a great Google review. Methodical, asks a million questions but in a good way.
last_interaction_summary: He texted asking if I know a good landscaper for their garden. I said I'd ask Damian.
notes: Told him I'd find a landscaper recommendation. That was almost a year ago. He probably found one himself by now but I never followed up. Embarrassing.

name: Oliwia Pawłowska
relationship: interior designer, collaborated on one luxury staging project in 2024
source: found her through Instagram, her portfolio was stunning
context: Young designer, very talented, studied in Milan. We did one high-end staging together for a listing on Kanonicza — it sold in three days at above asking. She's picky about what projects she takes on. Also does restaurant interiors.
last_interaction_summary: Coffee at Karma — I told her about a premium listing coming up on Floriańska. She's interested in staging it. Also her mother's assisted living move is happening this summer, the Wieliczka house will likely sell.
tags: interior designer, luxury staging, Milan-trained, premium
notes: Her mother lives alone in Wieliczka and might need to move to assisted living — Oliwia mentioned it in passing. Could mean a sale eventually. Also: she'd be perfect for Patryk Sobczak's new restaurant if he ever gets going.

name: Frank Weber
relationship: German property investor, met once at MIPIM in Cannes, 2025
source: exchanged cards at the networking reception
context: Works for a family office in Frankfurt that's looking to diversify into CEE residential. We talked for 20 minutes — he was specifically interested in Kraków and Wrocław. Seemed serious, but these conference connections often go nowhere.
last_interaction_summary: We exchanged two emails after MIPIM. His assistant asked for a market overview. I sent a one-pager. No reply.

name: Dominika Baran
relationship: works in the city urban planning department, met at a charity gala
source: sat at the same table at the WOŚP charity auction in January 2025
context: She's a spatial planning specialist at the city office. Fascinating conversation about upcoming zoning changes in Zabłocie and Płaszów. Could be incredibly useful to know what's coming before it's public — not insider trading, just being informed about city plans.
last_interaction_summary: The charity gala. We exchanged numbers. She said to call anytime if I have zoning questions. I never called.
tags: urban planning, zoning, city office, Zabłocie, Płaszów

name: Filip Maj
relationship: nephew of Damian Krawczyk, looking for real estate career advice
source: Damian asked me to talk to him at a family barbecue in 2025
context: Early 20s, just graduated, vaguely interested in real estate but also considering finance. Seemed smart but unfocused. I gave him 15 minutes of advice.
last_interaction_summary: Damian's barbecue. Brief chat while flipping burgers.

name: Laura Marchetti
relationship: Italian architect, met at a design fair in Łódź
source: Łódź Design Festival 2024, she was presenting adaptive reuse projects
context: Based in Turin, does projects across Europe. She's working on a factory-to-loft conversion in Łódź and was curious about the Kraków market. We exchanged cards and had a 30-minute coffee. Beautiful portfolio — industrial heritage conversions.
last_interaction_summary: Coffee at the design fair. She followed me on Instagram afterward.
tags: architect, Italian, adaptive reuse, industrial, design

name: Bartłomiej Czajkowski
relationship: property developer from Warsaw, LinkedIn connection
source: he reached out on LinkedIn after reading my post about secondary market pricing
context: Builds larger projects in Warsaw — 50-100 unit blocks. He's been looking at Kraków market for expansion. Very active on LinkedIn, writes long posts about market trends. Met for one coffee when he was in town.
last_interaction_summary: Coffee when he was in Kraków. He talked about Polish PropTech startups he's advising and asked about local construction costs.

name: Sylwia Jasińska
relationship: friend of a friend, met at Tomasz Barański's birthday party
source: Tomek's birthday dinner in Kielce, October 2025
context: Works as a headhunter specializing in executive placement at Hays. We chatted briefly about the property market — she's renting and might buy. Lives in Kraków despite the Kielce connection.
last_interaction_summary: Tomek's birthday party. Pleasant conversation over wine. She mentioned she might look for an apartment next year.
tags: headhunter, Hays, potential buyer, Tomek's friend
notes: Said maybe end of 2026. Soft contact. Worth a check-in around then. Might also be useful for executive clients if she ever hires C-levels relocating to Kraków.

name: Erik van den Berg
relationship: Dutch businessman, brief encounter at MIPIM 2025
source: same MIPIM conference in Cannes where I met Frank Weber
context: Runs a sustainable housing development company in the Netherlands. We talked for 10 minutes about green building standards in Poland vs. Western Europe. He gave me his card, said his company is interested in JVs in Poland.
last_interaction_summary: Quick chat at the MIPIM cocktail reception. I wrote on his card: 'green housing - JV interest.'
tags: Dutch, sustainable housing, green building, MIPIM

name: Weronika Stępień
relationship: journalist at Gazeta Krakowska, interviewed me for a property market article
source: she called the office looking for a local agent's perspective
context: Wrote a piece about rising apartment prices in Kraków in late 2024 and I was one of her sources. The article was fair and gave me decent visibility. She's mid-30s, sharp, covers business and economy for the local paper.
last_interaction_summary: The article was published. She emailed a thank-you and the link. I shared it on my socials.
tags: journalist, media, Gazeta Krakowska, visibility, PR
notes: She could be useful for future press — whenever I have a market take or a big sale. Should keep the relationship warm.
